threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to wait for the 243 at 3 am without an umbrella, poor soul. Rain here doesn't mess around.
Caught a night bus through Brixton and the driver stopped to help a lost dog find its owner. Only in South London do you see that kind of heart on a late shift.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted the elderly man feeding the swans at the canal, while a group of kids played on skateboards nearby. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a pop-up café that doubles as a gallery, showcasing local artists while you sip on a bio vegan latte.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows that dodging the BART delays is a skill, especially after that train brake meltdown last Friday. We’ve seen it all.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus today, and a street performer jumped on board to serenade us. Only in SF does public transit feel like a surprise concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the plaçes, but it’s the narrow streets after 9 PM that reveal the real vibe—family dinners and laughter, no tourists allowed.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of street performers like it's a novelty, while the locals barely glance up from their phones.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand at bus stops checking the arrival board, not realizing a live update on your phone is more reliable than half the info on those screens.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over who left the wine behind. Turns out, the bottle rolled under the seat, still half-full. Classic Brixton night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped into a café and overheard the barista arguing passionately about Kreuzberg's 'real' döner. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop open at 3 AM with a DJ spinning vinyl. It's like a late-night club for your tastebuds!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they react to a BART closure—it's a resigned sigh, not panic, like they've been through this chaos too many times.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 bus today, and a guy was singing opera at the stop. It was so beautiful, even the pigeons stopped to listen.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the plaça, but miss the real charm in shadowy alleys like Carrer de Verdi, where locals sip café con leche and actually talk.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they always stop to take selfies at the same Gaudí buildings, oblivious to the locals just passing by.
